caBundleSecretRef.description: Optional Secret in the cert-manager namespace carrying the Vault server's CA bundle (PEM under the configured key). cert-manager uses it to verify the Vault server's TLS. Typically `{ name: vault-pki-ca, key: ca.crt }` — the Secret produced by argocd.create-vault-issuer or by the AppSet itself.

auth.description: How the ClusterIssuer authenticates to Vault. With method "token" a static Secret-backed token is used, which nothing renews and which Vault caps at 768h — prefer "kubernetes", where cert-manager mints a short-lived ServiceAccount token per request. Both sub-objects may be present; only the one matching `method` is rendered, which lets the ApplicationSet pass both unconditionally (bare-line conditionals in helm.valuesObject break kustomize build).

method.description: Defaults to "token" when absent or empty, so existing callers are unaffected. The empty string is allowed because ApplicationSet templating renders a missing cluster annotation as "".

tokenSecretRef.description: Used when method is "token". Values may be empty strings when method is "kubernetes".

kubernetes.description: Used when method is "kubernetes". Values may be empty strings when method is "token".

mountPath.description: Vault Kubernetes auth mount including the /v1/auth/ prefix, e.g. "/v1/auth/my-cluster-certmanager".

role.description: Vault role name on that mount.

serviceAccountRef.description: ServiceAccount cert-manager presents when logging in. Defaults to the role name when empty.

destination.description: Inert pass-through. Present so verify-catalog's probe values do not fail strict additionalProperties:false validation; the chart itself does not consume this field.
